symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness ('like it's exploding') after a major life event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chest tightness is a common somatic feature of an adrenergic panic response.
New chest tightness mandates cardiac exclusion (ECG/exam) before attribution to stress — not achievable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sustained resting tachycardia (~110 bpm, patient wearable) that will not settle

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sinus tachycardia is expected from sympathetic overdrive.
A persistently high rate needs an ECG to separate sinus tachycardia from a pathological arrhythmia.
Tachycardia is consistent with thyrotoxicosis but non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Approximately 48 hours of total insomnia with agitation and hyperarousal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe insomnia and agitation are core to acute autonomic hyperarousal.
Reduced need for sleep occurs in mania, but here the affect is fearful rather than elevated.

symptoms.symptom_name: Positive life-event trigger (promotion) with survivor guilt, imposter fear and lost-years grief

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A tight temporal and psychological link between a threatening-feeling life change and symptom onset supports a stress reaction.
A clear psychological trigger lowers but does not eliminate cardiac probability.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible fine tremor, pacing and agitation (video-observable)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Fine tremor and psychomotor agitation are consistent with an adrenaline surge; visible on video, patient-side.
Fine tremor also occurs in thyrotoxicosis — non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of crushing / radiating chest pain; breathing subjectively normal; SpO2 99%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reassuring features lower probability but cannot exclude ACS, which can present atypically.
Elevated BP reflects the adrenergic surge — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI can only infer activation from the patient-reported racing heart.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal heart sounds and regular rhythm on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A regular rhythm on auscultation lowers arrhythmia probability — a clinician-only finding, sealed.

investigation_details.expected_result: Sinus tachycardia without ischaemic changes anticipated, but the test is required precisely to confirm this
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An acute coronary syndrome or a pathological tachyarrhythmia could be missed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An ECG is the first-line test to exclude ACS and characterise the tachycardia — unavailable in telehealth.

investigation_details.expected_result: Anticipated normal given the acute stressor-linked onset, but required to exclude thyrotoxicosis
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: An occult thyrotoxicosis could be attributed to stress.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: TFTs are needed to exclude a thyroid cause of the tachycardia and tremor.

symptoms.symptom_name: No suicidal ideation or self-harm intent on sensitive screening

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative safety screen at presentation sets the baseline tier below crisis level, but the screen must be performed and repeated if the picture changes.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Tachycardia, severe insomnia and tremor together describe a sympathetic surge — strongly supportive of a stress reaction ONCE organic mimics are excluded.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line to exclude ACS and characterise the sustained tachycardia — the exclusion telehealth cannot deliver.
Excludes thyrotoxicosis as a cause of the tachycardia and tremor.
Part of same-day in-person cardiac exclusion where the chest tightness and tachycardia warrant it.
